audience_note: Claims chosen for a VC reader: market size, funding flows, incumbent response, unit economics, and the 'replace SaaS seats' thesis must be sourced or killed — not asserted.

todos.claim: Enterprise agent spending is crossing from pilot budgets into production line items in 2026
The agent / orchestration layer is attracting outsized venture funding vs. raw model bets
Foundation models are commoditizing — price-per-token has collapsed, so margin moves up the stack
Reliability, not capability, is the binding constraint — multi-step agent task success is still the gating metric
A named enterprise is running agents in production with a quantified outcome (the proof-point example)
Incumbent SaaS vendors are repricing toward outcome/agent-based pricing in response
The 'agents replace SaaS seats' thesis — pressure-test it; is it real or vaporware?
Where the defensible moat sits in the stack (the investable layer)

source: content-research-writer step 2 — Research To-Do subsection (Find data / Get examples / Source citation, one per load-bearing claim)
